best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-trip-001 title: user starts a trip and completes it module: trip source pages: 2,3 preconditions: user is logged in and on the home page. test data: valid trip details. test steps: 1. user is on the home page. 2. user selects to start a trip. 3. application sends trip details to the server. 4. server processes the trip details and stores them in the database. 5. server confirms the trip has started. 6. user completes the trip. 7. application sends a completion signal to the server. 8. server updates the trip status to completed. 9. application informs the user that the trip is completed. 10. application prompts the user to calculate emissions. expected result: user is redirected to the tragitto terminato state.

item_text: the diagram models a state machine representing the flow of a trip calculation process.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-trip-001 title: user starts a trip and completes it module: trip source pages: 2,3 preconditions: user is logged in and on the home page. test data: valid trip details. test steps: 1. user is on the home page. 2. user selects to start a trip. 3. application sends trip details to the server. 4. server processes the trip details and stores them in the database. 5. server confirms the trip has started. 6. user completes the trip. 7. application sends a completion signal to the server. 8. server updates the trip status to completed. 9. application informs the user that the trip is completed. 10. application prompts the user to calculate emissions. expected result: user is redirected to the tragitto terminato state.

item_text: the process starts at the home state.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-access-001 title: user logs in successfully for the first time module: access source pages: 1 preconditions: user has never accessed the app before. test data: valid username and password. test steps: 1. user opens the app. 2. user is prompted to enter credentials. 3. user enters valid username and password. 4. application sends credentials to the server. 5. server verifies credentials against the database. 6. server confirms valid credentials. 7. application compares entered credentials with those in the database. 8. application informs the user that the login was successful. expected result: user is redirected to the home page.

best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-sequence-004 title: user starts a trip but skips emission calculation module: emission calculation source pages: 6 preconditions: user is logged in and on the home page. test data: valid trip details. test steps: 1. user is on the home page. 2. user selects to start a trip. 3. application sends trip details to the server. 4. server processes the trip details and stores them in the database. 5. server confirms the trip has started. 6. user completes the trip. 7. application sends a completion signal to the server. 8. server updates the trip status to completed. 9. application prompts the user to calculate emissions. 10. user chooses not to calculate emissions. 11. application returns to the home page. expected result: user is redirected to the home page.

item_text: if the user is registered, the user is prompted to enter login details.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-login-001 title: user successfully logs in module: login/registration source pages: 3.0 preconditions: user is not logged in. test data: - email: test@example.com - password: testpassword test steps: 1. user enters their email and password. 2. system checks if the user exists in the database. 3. system verifies the password using bcrypt. 4. system generates a jwt token. 5. system revokes any existing tokens for the user. 6. system inserts a new token into the database. 7. system sends the jwt token to the client. expected result: user is logged in and receives a valid jwt token.

item_text: the login details are then checked for correctness.

item_text: if the details are incorrect, the user is prompted to re-enter the login details.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-login-002 title: user fails to log in due to incorrect credentials module: login/registration source pages: 3.0 preconditions: user is not logged in. test data: - email: test@example.com - password: wrongpassword test steps: 1. user enters their email and password. 2. system checks if the user exists in the database. 3. system verifies the password using bcrypt. 4. system returns an error message. expected result: user receives an error message indicating incorrect credentials.
